configure-and-initialize-a-repository.label: Initialize the Community Project Repository
configure-and-initialize-a-repository.summary: Turn community-project into a repository on main and configure the supplied identity locally.

sketch0.title: An Ordinary Folder Becomes a Repository

sketch1.title: Repository Metadata Stays Behind the Scenes

try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.title: Initialize the Community Project
try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.prompt: The terminal opens inside `community-project/`. It is an ordinary folder with five small text files and no Git history. Initialize this folder as a repository whose first branch is `main`. Do not edit or stage any file yet.
try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.hint: Initialize the current project root and choose `main` as part of the same action.

help.concept: git init creates local repository metadata; `-b main` names the initial branch.
help.hint_1: The terminal is already inside community-project, so do not create or enter another folder.
help.hint_2: This step changes repository metadata only.

try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.instructions: The terminal opens inside `community-project/`. It is an ordinary folder with five small text files and no Git history. Initialize this folder as a repository whose first branch is `main`. Do not edit or stage any file yet.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Old planning notes

Spring flyer draft.
' > community-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project/generated
printf %s 'Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.
' > community-project/generated/summary.txt

try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.title: Store the Local Author Name
try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.prompt: The repository now exists. Store the supplied author name `ZoeSkoul Learner` in this repository only. Do not change the machine-wide Git configuration.
try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.hint: Use repository-local configuration with the exact supplied name.

help.concept: A local identity applies only to the current repository.
help.hint_1: Keep the quotation marks around the name because it contains a space.
help.hint_2: Do not use --global in this course workspace.

try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.instructions: The repository now exists. Store the supplied author name `ZoeSkoul Learner` in this repository only. Do not change the machine-wide Git configuration.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
git -C community-project init -q -b main
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Old planning notes

Spring flyer draft.
' > community-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project/generated
printf %s 'Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.
' > community-project/generated/summary.txt

' > community-project/old-notes.txt

try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.title: Complete and Verify the Local Identity
try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.prompt: Store `learner@zoeskoul.local` as the repository-local author email. Then read the local configuration and confirm that both the supplied name and email are present.
try_configure_and_initialize_a_repositor.hint: Add the email to the same local scope, then inspect that scope.

help.concept: Git records an author identity with each commit; this course uses a safe repository-only identity.
help.hint_2: The verification command should show both user.name and user.email.

# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
git -C community-project init -q -b main
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Old planning notes

Spring flyer draft.
' > community-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project/generated
printf %s 'Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.
' > community-project/generated/summary.txt
git -C community-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'

mkdir -p -- community-project/generated

Created automatically from project notes.

1.message: Did you read the repository-local configuration to verify both values?

single-choice-1.title: Repository-Local Identity
single-choice-1.prompt: Why does this course use `git config --local` for the supplied author identity?
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Turn an ordinary project folder into a Git repository on `main`, inspect the metadata Git created, and keep the author identity local to that repository.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: It changes the identity only inside the current repository.
options.b: It deletes any existing project history.
options.c: It stages every file in the project.
options.d: It changes the contents of the project files.

single-choice-2.title: Repository Initialization
single-choice-2.prompt: What command is used to initialize a new Git repository?
single-choice-2.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Turn an ordinary project folder into a Git repository on `main`, inspect the metadata Git created, and keep the author identity local to that repository.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

drag-reorder-1.title: Order the First Repository Setup
drag-reorder-1.prompt: Arrange the setup steps in an order that can actually work.
drag-reorder-1.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Turn an ordinary project folder into a Git repository on `main`, inspect the metadata Git created, and keep the author identity local to that repository.
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

tokens.t1: Initialize the project folder as a repository

fill-blank-choice-1.title: Git Identity Configuration
fill-blank-choice-1.prompt: Complete the command to set the local Git user name: `git config --local user.name [blank1]`
fill-blank-choice-1.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Turn an ordinary project folder into a Git repository on `main`, inspect the metadata Git created, and keep the author identity local to that repository.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

sketch0.title: An Ordinary Folder Becomes a Repository
sketch0.bodyMarkdown: `community-project/` begins as an ordinary folder containing five readable text files. Initializing the project adds local Git metadata without changing those files. The terminal already opens at the project root, so there is no folder-navigation puzzle.

sketch1.title: Repository Metadata Stays Behind the Scenes
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: Git stores its internal records in `.git/`. ZoeSkoul hides that internal directory from the Explorer. You work through Git commands instead of opening or editing metadata by hand.

sketch2.title: Identity Belongs to This Repository
sketch2.bodyMarkdown: A commit records an author name and email. Repository-local configuration keeps the supplied course identity inside `community-project/` and avoids changing the learner’s machine-wide settings. It is not a GitHub account.
